channel.title: Hafsteinn Einarsson
channel.description: Notes on computational neuroscience, NLP, and applied ML.

item.title: Bootstrap percolation
item.description: A graph-based process that models the spread of activity in a network, with an interactive vis.js simulation on the Erdős–Rényi random graph.

item.title: Asynchronous bootstrap percolation
item.description: Bootstrap percolation without synchronous rounds — when edge delays are random the activity percolates much faster, in constant expected time.

item.title: Percolation with inhibition - part 2
item.description: Modelling the percolation-with-inhibition process as a Markov chain on the potential of a single vertex, with an interactive D3 visualisation of the chain, a histogram of stopping times, and a running estimate of the probability of activation.

item.title: Leaky integrate and fire neurons (LIF)
item.description: A simple, widely-used membrane-voltage model of biological neurons. We motivate it from Hodgkin and Huxley's experiments, write down the differential equations, and explore the dynamics with an interactive D3 simulation.

item.title: Percolation with inhibition - part 1
item.description: Adding inhibitory vertices to bootstrap percolation. An interactive simulation with green and red nodes shows how competing positive and negative messages shape activity in the graph.

item.title: Turning recorded D&D sessions into comics
item.description: A small pipeline that records a session on a Zoom H1 Essential, transcribes the Icelandic audio with ElevenLabs, and renders each session as a graphic novel with gpt-image-2 and a set of reference images that keep the cast looking consistent across pages.
